description: Offline museum path for ?demo=1. Matches public/demo-museum/artifact.jpg and label.jpg. Person B: return vision → durations → timeline sections when DEMO_MODE or ?demo=1.

vision.labelText: National Museum of Colombo. Artifact: Standing Buddha sculpture. Period: c. 2nd-3rd century CE. Material: Stone. Kingdom: Anuradhapura era. Location: Colombo, Sri Lanka. Ancient Sri Lankan stone sculpture displayed in the archaeology gallery.
vision.historicalContext: Stone Buddha from the early Buddhist kingdom of Anuradhapura, representing the fusion of Indian Buddhist art with indigenous Sri Lankan craftsmanship during the island's first great capital.

options.label: 25 years
options.description: Immediate regional ripples across the island

options.label: 50 years
options.description: One generation — monastic and royal succession shifts

options.label: 75 years
options.description: Generational transformation of Buddhist patronage and trade

options.label: 150 years
options.description: From late Anuradhapura toward Polonnaruwa's rise

options.label: 300 years
options.description: Centuries of hydraulic and monastic expansion

events.title: Colombo shrine network expands
events.description: Patronage from the western coast accelerates new stupas and image houses modeled on the museum Buddha's style.

events.title: Monastic councils standardize iconography
events.description: Island-wide councils fix sculptural canons, spreading Anuradhapura-era forms before the Chola pressures of later centuries.

events.title: Pearl and spice routes pivot inland
events.description: Royal storehouses near ancient capitals hoard revenue that might otherwise flow to competing ports.

events.title: Hydraulic charters precede Polonnaruwa
events.description: Early tank commissions foreshadow the irrigation golden age, binding villages to monastic estates.

lostToHistory: Uniform colonial cataloguing of Sinhalese bronzes
Several minor coastal chiefdoms absorbed without record
gainedByHumanity: A pan-island sculptural tradition preserved in living monasteries
Earlier written chronicles of Anuradhapura patronage
timeline.relicPrompt: Museum photograph of a weathered 3rd-century Sri Lankan stone Buddha torso, soft gallery lighting, archival label card, realistic archaeological artifact.
